data.title: DATA Communications Management Posts Higher Profit, Lower Revenue, for the Fourth Quarter
data.description:
DATA Communications Management after trade Wednesday said its fourth-quarter profit rose while revenue declined year-over-year.

The company earned $2.57 million, or $0.04 per share, in the period, up from $1.36 million, or $0.02, a year ago. FactSet expected per share earnings of $0.06.

Revenue fell to $116.2 million from $130 million in the year-ago quarter. FactSet consensus was $116.9 million.

The company reaffirmed its long-term growth target for the next five years, aiming for a compound annual growth rate of more than 5% in revenue, a gross profit margin of over 30% of revenues, and an adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization margin of more than 14% each year.

"While we are pleased with our start to 2025, we continue to carefully monitor economic conditions and the geopolitical environment for developments that could impact our results. These include the recent introduction of cross-border tariffs, raw material cost increases and any softening of demand in our end markets. We are actively pursuing opportunities to mitigate against these risks, including initiatives to diversify our supply chain," Chief Executive Richard Kellam said.

The company's shares closed up $0.01 to $1.97 on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

data.title: N-able Approves $75 Million Stock Repurchase Program
data.description:
N-able said late Wednesday its board approved a $75 million stock repurchase plan with no expiration date.

Shares of the company were up about 2.1% in recent after-hours activity.

data.title: Xtract One Announces Second Quarter Fiscal 2025 Results
data.description:
TORONTO, March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Xtract One Technologies Inc. (OTCQX: XTRAF) (FRA: 0PL) (“Xtract One” or the “Company”) a leading technology-driven threat detection and security solution that prioritizes the patron access experience by leveraging AI, today announced fiscal second quarter results for the three months ended January 31, 2025. All information is in Canadian dollars unless otherwise indicated.

Second Quarter Highlights

  • Quarterly revenue of $3.4 million for the three months ended January 31, 2025 versus $2.9 million in the prior-year period.
  • Gross margin of 70% for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 versus 61% in the prior-year period.
  • Total contract value of new bookings1 was $13.5 million for the three months ending January 31, 2025 – the strongest quarterly results in the Company’s history – as compared to $5.1 million for the same period last year.
  • Contractual backlog was $16.7 million at the end of the second quarter as compared to $12.3 million in the prior-year period, excluding an additional $20.5 million of agreements pending installation1 versus approximately $10.0 million at the end of the second quarter of fiscal 2024.

“By booking a record $13.5 million of orders, we ended the period with $37.2 million in total backlog – a new level for the Company – strengthening our outlook for the balance of fiscal 2025 and beyond and putting us on track for our best year ever” stated Peter Evans, Chief Executive Officer of Xtract One. “We are very pleased to be experiencing revenue growth despite second quarter results being tempered by deployment timing and a larger than typical percentage of subscription contracts. We are also pleased that we continue to win new contracts in multiple industry verticals and geographies – another strategic step in accelerating market penetration and acceptance, and diversifying the business. At the same time, we are seeing significant interest in our Xtract One Gateway solution. The outlook for the second half of fiscal 2025 remains bright, and we are well positioned on the path to profitability.”

Financial Results for the Three Month Period Ended January 31, 2025

Consolidated revenue was $3.4 million for the three months ended January 31, 2025 as compared to $2.9 million for the same period last year, reflecting new business wins and a greater number of installations. Gross profit was $2.4 million, or a gross profit margin of 70%, in the fiscal 2025 second quarter versus $1.8 million, or a gross profit margin of 61%, in the prior-year period.

Comprehensive loss was $2.1 million for the three month period ended January 31, 2025 as compared to $3.3 million for the same period in fiscal 2024. This reflects higher revenue and gross profit as well as lower research and development expenses.

This press release should be read in conjunction with the Company’s Unaudited Condensed Consolidated Interim Financial Statements, prepared in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (“IFRS”) and the Company’s Management’s Discussion and Analysis for the three and six month periods ended January 31, 2025 and 2024, which can be found on the Company’s website and under the Company’s profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

Conference Call Details

Xtract One will host a conference call to discuss its results tomorrow, March 13, 2025 at 10:00 am EST. Peter Evans, Xtract One CEO and Director, and Karen Hersh, CFO and Corporate Secretary, will provide an overview of the interim financial results along with management’s outlook for the business, followed by a question-and-answer period.

The webcast and presentation will be accessible on the company’s website. The webcast can be accessed here and the telephone number for the conference call is 844-481-3016 (412-317-1881 for international callers).

About Xtract One Technologies

Xtract One Technologies is a leading technology-driven threat detection and security solution leveraging AI to provide seamless and secure patron access control experiences. The Company makes unobtrusive weapons and threat detection systems that are designed to assist facility operators in prioritizing- and delivering improved “Walk-right-In” experiences while enhancing safety. Xtract One's innovative portfolio of AI-powered Gateway solutions excels at allowing facilities to discreetly screen and identify weapons and other threats at points of entry and exit without disrupting the flow of traffic. With solutions built to serve the unique market needs for schools, hospitals, arenas, stadiums, manufacturing, distribution, and other customers, Xtract One is recognized as a market leader delivering the highest security in combination with the best individual experience. For more information, visit www.xtractone.com or connect on Facebook, Twitter, and LinkedIn. 

About Threat Detection and Security Solutions

Xtract One solutions, when properly configured, deployed, and utilized, are designed to help enhance safety and reduce threats. Given the wide range of potential threats in today's world, no threat detection system is 100% effective. Xtract One solutions should be utilized as one element in a multilayered approach to physical security.

For further information, please contact:

Xtract One Inquiries: info@xtractone.com, http://www.xtractone.com    

Media Contact: Kristen Aikey, JMG Public Relations, 212-206-1645, kristen@jmgpr.com

Investor Relations: Chris Witty, Darrow Associates, 646-438-9385, cwitty@darrowir.com

1 Supplementary Financial Measures:

The Company utilizes specific supplementary financial measures in this earnings release to allow for a better evaluation of the operating performance of the Company’s business and facilitates meaningful comparison of results in the current period with those in prior periods and future periods. Supplementary financial measures do not have any standardized meaning prescribed under IFRS and therefore may not be comparable to measures presented by other companies. Supplementary financial measures presented in this earnings release include ‘Agreements pending installation’ and ‘Total contract value of new bookings.’ Agreements pending installation reflects total value of signed contracts awarded to the Company that has not been installed at the customer site. ‘Total contract value of new bookings’ is comprised of all new contracts signed and awarded to the Company, regardless of the performance obligations outstanding as of the end of the reporting period. Total contract value is the aggregate value of sales commitments from customers as at the end of the reporting period without consideration of the Company’s completion of the associated performance obligations outlined in each contract.

data.title: North America coffee industry seeks answers amid trade war
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trade war could impact coffee operations across North America
  • Industry fears potential tariffs on South American coffee imports
  • Companies have roasting, packaging, trading bases in U.S. and Canada

North American coffee market participants are seeking clarity on how the trade war started by the administration of U.S. President Donald Trump impacts their businesses across the region, where operations are highly interconnected.

Companies have roasting, packaging and trading bases in both the United States and Canada in order to better supply clients. Potential U.S. tariffs and any retaliatory ones will now have to be considered when deciding what to make, and where, industry participants said.

Mexico, meanwhile, is a regular supplier of high-quality green coffee to both the U.S. and Canada, as well as an exporter of instant coffee.

"There are operations across the (U.S.-Canada) border for roasting and supplying retail channels, on both sides," said the CEO of one of the largest coffee companies in the region, who asked not to be named due to the sensitivity of the issue.

Starbucks , for example, roasts the coffee used in its hundreds of Canadian stores in the United States.

"As the political environment moves or the tariff markets move, we will figure out how to navigate that effectively given those conditions in the regulatory environments," said Starbucks Chief Executive Brian Niccol in a meeting with shareholders on Wednesday.

The postponement of U.S. tariffs of 25% on the majority of goods from Canada and Mexico does not seem to have included most of the forms of coffee traded, according to documentation seen by Reuters, because the product is mostly absent from the USMCA, the free trade agreement between the U.S., Mexico and Canada.

"We have added a clause in our contracts saying the buyer would pay the additional 25% tariff if it is considered due. Most traders are doing that," said Jeff Bernstein, managing director of coffee trader RGC, based in Quebec, Canada.

"We are exporting some coffee from Mexico to a client in Oakland (California), and he agreed with the clause," he said.

Switzerland's Nestle has invested heavily in instant coffee operations in Mexico in recent years, including a program with thousands of farmers to boost production of robusta coffee, the main raw material.

Nestle did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Bill Murray, president of the U.S. National Coffee Association, said coffee should be exempt from additional tariffs.

"Tariffs on coffee would impact three in four Americans. Many think exports, not imports, are good for America, but unfortunately we cannot grow coffee in the U.S."

There is also fear in the industry about possible U.S. tariffs on South American countries, where most imported coffee comes from.

"Trump, in one of his speeches about tariffs, mentioned Brazil, although superficially. Brazil is on the radar," said Andre Acosta, director of Commodity Solutions Latam for broker Marex.

Brazil is the world's largest coffee producer and exporter.

Brazilian Vice President Geraldo Alckmin and U.S. Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick met last week and officials have started consultations on trade policy.

data.title: Australia shares set to open slightly higher; NZ flat
data.description:
The Australian stock market was set to inch higher at open on Thursday, as investors pivoted their focus to an encouraging inflation update from the U.S. which bolstered the view for early interest rate cuts, shrugging off fears of a global trade war.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) rose 0.2%, a 12.8-point premium to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark slipped 1.3% to close at a seven-month low on Wednesday.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index was little changed by 2121 GMT.

data.title: Discount on Western Canada Select heavy crude narrows
data.description:
The discount of Western Canada Select (WCS) heavy crude to the North American benchmark West Texas Intermediate futures (WTI) narrowed on Wednesday.

* WCS for April delivery in Hardisty, Alberta, settled at $10.90 a barrel under WTI, according to brokerage CalRock, after having settled at $11 under the U.S. benchmark on Tuesday.

* The Canadian heavy crude discount has been narrowing this week as the market reacted to the one-month tariff reprieve announced last Thursday by U.S. President Donald Trump. The White House had previously said energy products from Canada would be subject to a 10% tariff rate.

* Canada exports approximately 4 million barrels of oil per day, about 90% of its total crude exports, to the United States. Analysts say much of the cost of potential tariffs would be borne by Canadian oil producers in the form of lower prices for their product.

* Canada's energy minister Jonathan Wilkinson said on Tuesday that Canada could impose non-tariff measures such as restricting its oil exports to the United States or levying export duties on products if a trade dispute with the U.S. escalates further.

* Global oil prices rose 2% on Wednesday, as U.S. government data showed tighter-than-expected oil and fuel inventories. Investors continued to keep an eye on mounting fears of a U.S. economic slowdown and the impact of tariffs on global economic growth.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1717 ET - Intel names Lip-Bu Tan as its new top executive, effective March 18. Tan, who previously served on the beleaguered semiconductor company's board of directors, has more than 20 years of semiconductor and software experience, having formerly held the top role at Cadence Design Systems. At Cadence, Tan helped turn around the company, focusing on what Intel said was customer-centric innovation that helped double revenue, expand operating margins and send its stock higher. His appointment comes as Intel's core business of making chips for personal computers and servers has been challenged amid an artificial-intelligence boom that has largely benefited rival Nvidia. Shares rise 11% in after-hours trading. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1605 ET - The S&P 500 and Nasdaq rise after inflation slowed in February, though tariffs are threatening to raise some prices in the months ahead. Big technology and communication services companies lead the advance, while consumer staples are broadly weaker. Canada and the European Union announced retaliatory tariffs after the Trump administration's 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports took effect overnight. The DJIA falls 82 points, or 0.2%, to 41350, while the S&P 500 gains 0.5% to 5599 and the Nasdaq rises 1.2% to 17648. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

1522 ET - An ambitious C$7 billion battery plant in Quebec is on the line now that Northvolt filed for bankruptcy in Sweden. The company says that a series of compounding challenges "eroded its financial position" and it has been unable to secure any viable financial and operational future for the company. This puts into question the future of the Quebec battery plant, for which Northvolt received major financial commitments from all levels of Canadian government, including C$2.9 billion from Quebec, and about half that by the federal government, plus a number of other incentives. So far, early investments mean that taxpayers have lost a fraction of that, but losing sight of the promise of building a new industry of the future may be the bigger cost to the country. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1511 ET - Brown-Forman CEO Lawson Whiting believes that sales of spirits will rise once people have a bit more discretionary income. That is consumers are still buying booze--they are just currently trading down to smaller bottles that cost less, because their bank accounts have been squeezed by inflation, he says at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference. "They still want that affordable cocktail luxury at the end of the evening, but they're just switching down to a smaller size that's more affordable for them," Whiting says. "That's why we think a large part of this is cyclical and that it's going to turn." Shares fall 4.7%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1444 ET - Weight-loss drugs, weed and waning demand--particularly among Gen Z consumers--are what Brown-Forman CEO Lawson Whiting calls the big three dynamics hurting the U.S. spirits market. The rapid increase in the number of people that take GLP-1 agonists, or weight-loss drugs such as Ozempic and Wegovy, is meaningful, Whiting says: "We would be naive if we said that's not a long-term headwind." At the same time, cannabis use is on the rise, and younger consumers are drinking less. Whiting notes, though, that softer demand for spirits across Gen Z drinkers may be the result of economic inflation. That is because younger consumers are more often buying smaller portions of whiskey, which are less expensive, he says. Brown-Forman shares fall 5.3%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1438 ET - U.S. auto dealer views on electric vehicle sales improved in 1Q from the final quarter of 2024, but are still weak, Cox Automotive says in its latest Automotive Dealer Sentiment Index. The 1Q index indicates many dealers feel EV sales are worse now than a year ago, Cox says. Expectations for the EV market rose in 1Q, but the score of 40 shows most dealers expect the EV market will decline, rather than grow, over the next three months, according to Cox. The survey also shows dealers continue to view the EV tax credit favorably, citing its benefits for both dealerships and sales, Cox says. (stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com)

1431 ET - Labrador Iron Ore Royalty faces lower growth and a challenging tariff landscape, TD Cowen's Craig Hutchison says in a report, downgrading the stock to hold from buy. The analyst notes a 4Q earnings miss, though cash-flow per share was above TD estimates. He also mentions sputtering steel demand as a risk amid escalating tariffs and softer global growth. Shares are down 3.4% to C$29.15, and off 3.5% in the past 12 months. TD Cowen also lowers its price target to C$31 from C$32. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1421 ET - Front-month gold settles up 0.9% to $2,939.10 a troy ounce, making it the sixth session out of the past eight that front-month gold closed with a positive finish. The escalating tariff battle seen today — with the EU instituting retaliatory tariffs and Canada adding new levies — is helping propel gold, says Peter Cardillo of Spartan Capital Securities. "We believe gold is moving higher due to the prospects of the trade war intensifying in the coming weeks," he says. "The gold market is rising in response to potential inflation stemming from the trade war and uncertainties surrounding global economic activity."SPDR Gold rises 0.6%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1417 ET - Lawson Whiting, chief executive of Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman, says that the European Union's retaliatory tariffs on imports of American whiskey, among other goods, aren't a surprise. "There's been a lot of signals going on for a long time, particularly coming from the EU," he says at the UBS Global Consumer and Retail Conference. "It's a tough spot," Whiting says, adding that it is good news the tariffs don't go into effect until April 1. "We've got a few weeks to be able to try to get these parties together and resolve the differences on this particular tariff conversation," he says. "It does give me some optimism that both the administration and the other counterparties over the last few weeks have been trying to resolve things relatively quickly." Brown-Forman off more than 5%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1412 ET - The U.S. imposed 25% tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports earlier today, but President Trump had threatened a 50% tariff on Canadian steel and aluminum Tuesday, before relenting. That additional tariff would likely change trade flows, but not in a way that would benefit U.S. manufacturing, Alcoa CFO Molly Beerman says at the JPMorgan Industrials Conference. Under proposed higher tariffs, Alcoa would most likely send its aluminum produced in Canada to Europe, she says. "That supply may very well make its way over to the U.S.," Beerman says, adding that to have ships crossing the Atlantic with the same product just to get the right tariff treatment would be very disruptive. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1357 ET - Costco Wholesale's newest club opening in Massachusetts isn't closely surrounded by competitors, supporting the case that the club segment has room to grow, says D.A. Davidson's Michael Baker in a research note. Baker notes that the closest existing Costco by car is about 20 minutes from this new location, while the closest BJ's is a similar distance; Sam's Clubs, meanwhile, doesn't have a footprint in Massachusetts, with the closest one an hour away in New Hampshire. "This supports our view that the club segment has plenty of white space even with all three players in growth mode," says Baker. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1350 ET - The Public Company Accounting Oversight Board says it fined PricewaterhouseCoopers' Singapore unit $1.5 million for failing to have appropriate policies over testing to ensure that employees are free of conflicts of interest. Over nearly two years, employees in PwC Singapore's independence office understated the rates at which the firm failed to properly or timely report their financial interests and relationships, the regulator says. In some cases, staff misclassified certain failures as self-reported, the PCAOB says. The unit, which did not admit or deny the PCAOB's claims, agreed to work to improve how it monitors its independence processes. PwC Singapore said there was no breach of external independence rules and none of its clients were affected. "We are determined to learn from this and are committed to implementing measures to further strengthen compliance," the unit told WSJ. (mark.maurer@wsj.com; @markgmaurer)

data.title: PEOPLES BANCORP OF NORTH CAROLINA INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Peoples Bancorp of North Carolina, Inc., a bank holding company for Peoples Bank, has released its annual Form 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year. The report details the company's strategic initiatives, challenges, and future outlook, offering valuable insights into its business operations and market position.

Financial Highlights

While specific financial figures were not provided in the summary, the report references key financial performance metrics that can be found in the Annual Report. These metrics typically include:

  • Total Revenue: Reflecting the company's sales growth and overall financial health.
  • Gross Profit: Indicating the efficiency of the company's production and cost management.
  • Operating Income: Highlighting the company's operational efficiency and profitability.
  • Net Income: Showing the company's overall profitability after taxes and expenses.
  • Diluted EPS: Demonstrating the earnings per share, a key indicator of shareholder value.

Business Highlights

Peoples Bancorp of North Carolina, Inc. operates as a bank holding company for Peoples Bank, a state-chartered commercial bank founded in 1912. The Bank serves the Catawba Valley and surrounding communities through 16 banking offices and several loan production offices in North Carolina. Key business highlights include:

  • Market Area and Competition: The Bank's primary market area includes a 50-mile radius around its headquarters in Newton, North Carolina, encompassing several counties. The proximity to Charlotte, a major metropolitan area, significantly influences the Bank's market dynamics. The Bank faces strong competition from other banks, credit unions, and financial institutions in attracting deposits and making loans.
  • Loan Portfolio: The Bank maintains a diversified loan portfolio with a focus on real estate loans, particularly variable and fixed-rate commercial property loans. The portfolio is spread across various industries, with no significant concentration in any single industry.
  • Subsidiaries: The Bank has four subsidiaries providing investment services, real estate advisory, appraisal services, and real estate holdings. These subsidiaries enhance the Bank's service offerings and operational capabilities.
  • Human Capital Management: As of December 31, 2024, the Company employed 281 full-time and 13 part-time employees. The Company emphasizes diversity, inclusion, and professional development as part of its corporate culture.
  • Regulatory Environment: The Company and the Bank are subject to extensive regulation under federal and state laws, including the Dodd-Frank Act and the Economic Growth Act. These regulations impact the Bank's operations, capital requirements, and compliance obligations.
  • Investment Policies: The Bank's investment policy focuses on maintaining liquidity, maximizing earnings, and managing interest rate risk. The investment portfolio is managed to align with the Bank's broader business strategies and risk tolerances.
  • Cybersecurity and Privacy: The Bank employs a multi-layered cybersecurity approach to protect sensitive data and ensure business continuity. Compliance with federal privacy regulations is a key focus to safeguard customer information.
  • Community Reinvestment: The Bank is committed to meeting the credit needs of its community, receiving a 'satisfactory' rating in its last Community Reinvestment Act examination.
  • Future Outlook: The Bank anticipates continued growth in its core deposit base driven by economic growth in its market area and the introduction of competitive deposit products. The regulatory landscape is expected to evolve, impacting operational strategies.

Strategic Initiatives

The company is focused on maintaining a strong financial position by managing its liquidity and capital resources effectively. Key strategic initiatives include:

  • Capital Management: The company has a stock repurchase program authorized in June 2024, under which it repurchased 1,275 shares in the last quarter of 2024 for its deferred compensation plan. The repurchases were funded by participant contributions. The company also maintains a focus on shareholder returns, as evidenced by its ability to pay dividends, which are contingent upon the Bank's financial health and regulatory compliance. The company’s common stock is listed on the NASDAQ Global Market under the symbol 'PEBK'.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategic focus on capital management by leveraging its stock repurchase program and maintaining its dividend policy, subject to regulatory constraints. The company aims to enhance shareholder value through prudent financial management and strategic capital allocation.

Challenges and Risks

The company faces several significant risks, including:

  • Economic Conditions: Unfavorable economic conditions, such as recessionary trends, inflation, and changes in interest rates, could adversely affect the company's financial performance by increasing nonperforming loans and reducing demand for products and services.
  • Credit Risk: The company is exposed to credit risk, particularly in its commercial real estate and construction loan portfolios, which are susceptible to losses during economic downturns.
  • Concentration Risk: A large portion of the company's loans are concentrated in the Catawba Valley area, increasing vulnerability to local economic changes.
  • Interest Rate Risk: Changes in interest rates can affect the company's profitability by impacting the spread between interest earned on assets and interest paid on liabilities.
  • Cybersecurity: The company is at risk of cybersecurity incidents that could disrupt operations and result in the loss of critical information, impacting reputation and financial results.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to extensive regulation, which could impact operations and financial performance. Changes in laws and regulations, such as those related to the Dodd-Frank Act and the Economic Growth Act, could impose additional compliance costs.
  • Liquidity Risk: The company relies on customer deposits as a primary funding source. Any significant withdrawal of deposits could adversely affect liquidity and financial condition.
  • Competition: The company faces intense competition from larger financial institutions and non-bank entities, which could impact market share and profitability.

Management acknowledges these challenges and has implemented strategies to address them, including maintaining strong credit underwriting standards, diversifying the loan portfolio, and enhancing cybersecurity measures. The company also focuses on expanding its deposit base to mitigate liquidity risks.

data.title: HUDSON TECHNOLOGIES INC /NY SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Hudson Technologies, Inc., a leading refrigerant services company focused on environmentally sustainable practices, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a highly regulated industry.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $237.1 million, a decrease of $51.9 million or 18% from the $289.0 million reported during the comparable 2023 period, primarily due to lower selling prices of certain refrigerants sold.

Cost of Sales: $171.4 million or 72% of sales, compared to $177.5 million or 61% of sales in 2023, with the increase in percentage primarily due to lower revenue and additional inventory reserve.

Selling, General and Administrative Expenses: $33.0 million, an increase of $2.5 million from the $30.5 million reported during the comparable 2023 period, mainly due to increased personnel cost, professional fees, and acquisition costs.

Amortization Expense: $3.4 million, an increase of $0.6 million from $2.8 million reported during the comparable 2023 period, primarily due to the recent acquisition of USA Refrigerants.

Interest Expense (Income): ($0.5) million, compared to $8.4 million in 2023, due to the repayment of the remaining $32.5 million principal balance under its Term Loan Facility.

Other Income: $2.3 million, including proceeds from litigation settlement of $1.8 million and $0.5 million from the lease opt-out related to the Smyrna, Georgia facility lease.

Income Tax Expense: $7.6 million, compared to $17.6 million for 2023, determined by applying statutory income tax rates to pre-tax income after adjustments.

Net Income: $24.4 million, a decrease of $27.8 million from the $52.2 million of net income reported during the comparable 2023 period, primarily due to lower sales prices of certain refrigerants sold and higher SG&A costs.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: Hudson Technologies, Inc. is a refrigerant services company providing solutions for the refrigeration industry, focusing on environmentally sustainable practices from refrigerant sales to reclamation and disposal.

Regulatory Environment: The company operates in a highly regulated industry, with significant impacts from the Clean Air Act and the AIM Act, which influence the production and use of refrigerants.

Product and Service Offerings: Hudson offers a range of services including refrigerant reclamation, RefrigerantSide® Services, and industrial gas sales, primarily used in commercial air conditioning and refrigeration systems.

Sustainability Initiatives: The company emphasizes sustainability through its reclamation processes, reducing the need for new refrigerant production and supporting the circular economy.

Refrigerant Sales: Hudson sells both reclaimed and virgin refrigerants, including CFC, HCFC, HFC, and HFO types, with a focus on transitioning to lower global warming potential products.

Strategic Relationships: The company has strategic agreements with AprilAire and Lennox International to supply reclaimed refrigerants, aligning with California's CARB regulations.

Recent Acquisition: In June 2024, Hudson acquired USA Refrigerants, enhancing its customer network and geographic footprint in the U.S., and increasing access to refrigerants for reclamation.

Operational Challenges: The company faces challenges from regulatory changes, competition, and the need to source sufficient refrigerant quantities, impacting its operational performance.

Future Outlook: Hudson anticipates continued growth in its reclamation services and aims to expand its market presence through strategic acquisitions and partnerships.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company completed the acquisition of USA Refrigerants to expand its customer network and enhance its geographic footprint in the United States. This acquisition is expected to strengthen the company's refrigerant distribution capabilities and support growth in reclamation.

Capital Management: The company repaid in full the remaining principal balance under its Term Loan Facility during the third quarter of 2023. It also repurchased 1,244,076 shares of its common stock at a cost of $8.1 million. The company has an Amended Wells Fargo Facility that allows borrowing up to $75 million for working capital and other needs. Additionally, the company increased its share repurchase program to allow up to $20 million in repurchases over 2024 and 2025.

Future Outlook: The company plans to maintain its focus on liquidity and capital management by leveraging its Amended Wells Fargo Facility and continuing its share repurchase program. It aims to satisfy working capital requirements through anticipated cash flows from operations and available funds, while also exploring potential acquisitions and expansion opportunities.

Challenges and Risks

Regulatory and Market Risks: Hudson Technologies operates in a highly regulated industry, with its business substantially dependent on the sale and continued environmental regulation of refrigerants. Changes in government regulations, particularly those related to the emission and production of refrigerants, could materially affect the company's operations. The company is also exposed to risks related to the availability and price of refrigerants, which are influenced by regulatory changes and market conditions.

Financial and Operational Risks: Hudson faces significant risks related to fluctuating commodity prices, particularly for refrigerant gases, which can materially affect revenues and cash flows. The company's existing and future debt obligations could impair liquidity and financial condition, especially if loan covenants are violated. Customer concentration is a risk, with the United States Defense Logistics Agency accounting for a significant portion of revenues. Additionally, the company is exposed to cybersecurity threats that could disrupt operations and damage reputation. Regulatory compliance burdens are substantial, with potential liability for environmental damage and personal injury in the refrigerant recovery and reclamation industry.

Management Strategies: The company has identified lower selling prices of certain refrigerants as a significant challenge, impacting revenues and operating margins. The recent acquisition of USA Refrigerants is expected to provide broader customer access and enhance distribution capabilities, but integration risks remain. Management is focused on maintaining liquidity and meeting financial covenants under its credit facility, with a strategy to manage working capital through cash flows and available credit.

Market Risks: Hudson is exposed to market risks from interest rate fluctuations on its credit facility and from changes in the demand, price, and availability of refrigerants. The inability to source sufficient quantities of refrigerants or obtain them on commercially reasonable terms could lead to revenue reductions and inventory write-downs, adversely affecting financial results.

data.title: Assertio Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Assertio Holdings, Inc., a specialty pharmaceutical company, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's efforts to expand its product portfolio, improve financial metrics, and navigate a complex regulatory and competitive landscape.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $124.961 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year's $152.069 million, primarily due to lower product sales.
  • Loss from Operations: $(24.476) million, showing a significant improvement from the previous year's loss of $(243.536) million, driven by reduced impairment charges.
  • Net Loss and Comprehensive Loss: $(21.581) million, compared to $(331.942) million in the prior year, indicating a substantial reduction in losses.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Share: $(0.23), improved from $(4.67) in the previous year, reflecting the impact of reduced net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Product Portfolio Expansion: The company expanded its product portfolio through the acquisition of ROLVEDON, a long-acting myeloid growth factor, via the merger with Spectrum Pharmaceuticals, Inc. in July 2023. This product is designed to decrease the incidence of infection in adult patients receiving anti-cancer drugs.
  • Product Differentiation: Sympazan, acquired in October 2022, utilizes PharmFilm® technology to deliver clobazam in a convenient film form, differentiating it from other clobazam products and aiding patients with swallowing difficulties.
  • Sales and Marketing Strategy: The company employs a patient-centric approach with a sales force dedicated to ROLVEDON and an omni-channel promotional model for Sympazan and other products, integrating digital channels and machine learning to enhance market reach.
  • Manufacturing and Supply Chain: The company relies on contract manufacturing partners in the U.S., Canada, Italy, and South Korea for product supply. In 2024, quality issues with certain manufacturers led to inventory write-downs, impacting cost of sales.
  • Intellectual Property: The company holds U.S. and foreign patents for ROLVEDON, with U.S. patents expiring between 2031 and 2042, and biologic exclusivity in the U.S. until 2034. Patents for other products like Sympazan and Otrexup extend through 2040 and 2030, respectively.
  • Regulatory Compliance: The company is subject to FDA regulations and must maintain compliance with cGMP standards. It also faces potential legal challenges related to patent infringement and generic competition.
  • Competition: ROLVEDON faces competition from other long-acting G-CSFs and biosimilars in the U.S. market. INDOCIN products face generic competition and compounded alternatives, while Sympazan competes with other LGS treatments.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to expand its product portfolio through acquisitions and licensing, focusing on products that offer commercial synergies, significant patent life, and accretive margins. It also aims to resolve ongoing legal matters efficiently.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Acquisition of Spectrum Pharmaceuticals, Inc.: Assertio Holdings, Inc. completed the acquisition of Spectrum Pharmaceuticals, Inc., a strategic move to expand its product portfolio in the oncology sector. The acquisition was accounted for using the acquisition method under ASC 805, with a total fair value of consideration transferred amounting to $252.9 million. This included the issuance of common stock and contingent value rights (CVRs) tied to future sales milestones of Spectrum's product ROLVEDON.
  • Capital Management: The company managed its capital structure by maintaining its 6.5% Convertible Senior Notes due 2027, with a carrying balance of $38.8 million as of December 31, 2024. The notes allow for conversion into common stock, cash, or a combination thereof, providing flexibility in capital management. Assertio also engaged in a privately negotiated exchange of $30 million principal amount of these notes, incurring an induced conversion expense. Shareholders' equity was impacted by stock-based compensation and the issuance of common stock related to the Spectrum Merger, resulting in an increase in additional paid-in capital to $794.2 million.
  • Future Outlook: Assertio plans to continue leveraging its expanded product portfolio post-Spectrum acquisition to drive revenue growth, particularly focusing on the oncology market. The company aims to achieve sales milestones for ROLVEDON, which could trigger additional contingent payments. Additionally, Assertio is evaluating the potential impact of new accounting pronouncements on its financial disclosures, indicating a proactive approach to financial management and compliance.

Challenges and Risks

  • Sales and Reimbursement Risks: The company faces significant risks related to the sales of ROLVEDON, which depend heavily on coverage and reimbursement from third-party payors, including government programs like Medicare and Medicaid. Any reduction in coverage or reimbursement could materially affect product sales and financial results.
  • Supply Chain Risks: The company relies on a single qualified supplier for the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) in each of its products, posing a risk of supply chain disruptions.
  • Competition: The company is exposed to competition from generic versions of its products, such as INDOCIN and CAMBIA, which have already led to significant market share loss and pricing concessions. The introduction of additional generics could further impact revenues.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are heightened by ongoing governmental investigations and inquiries into the company's historical commercialization of opioids, which could result in significant liabilities and reputational harm. Changes in laws and increased scrutiny of the pharmaceutical industry, particularly the opioid market, could adversely affect the company's operations.
  • Market Risks: The company is subject to market risks, including fluctuations in the market price of its common stock, which has been volatile. This volatility could impact the trading price of its convertible notes and affect the company's ability to raise capital. Additionally, the company faces risks related to its significant indebtedness, which could limit its ability to fund operations or strategic transactions.
  • Future Outlook: Management has identified the need to successfully grow ROLVEDON sales as a critical focus, given its status as the lead product. The company is also actively pursuing business development strategies, including acquisitions and strategic partnerships, to enhance its product offerings and market position. However, the success of these strategies is uncertain and depends on various factors, including the availability of attractive opportunities and the ability to integrate acquired businesses or products effectively.

SEC Filing: Assertio Holdings, Inc. [ ASRT ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2025

data.title: Ares Acquisition Corp II SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Ares Acquisition Corporation II, a blank check company formed to effectuate a merger, share exchange, asset acquisition, share purchase, reorganization, or similar business combination, has released its Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces as it seeks to complete its initial business combination.

Financial Highlights

Net Income: Ares Acquisition Corp II reported a net income of $25.99 million for the year ended December 31, 2024, primarily driven by investment income earned on investments held in the Trust Account. For the year ended December 31, 2023, the company reported a net income of $16.92 million, also primarily driven by investment income earned on investments held in the Trust Account.

Net Income Per Share: While not explicitly stated, the net income is shared pro rata between Class A and Class B ordinary shares.

Business Highlights

Business Model: Ares Acquisition Corporation II is a blank check company, also known as a Special Purpose Acquisition Company (SPAC), formed to effectuate a business combination with one or more businesses. The company has not engaged in any operations or generated revenue to date, classifying it as a 'shell company' under the Exchange Act.

Initial Public Offering: The company completed its Initial Public Offering (IPO) on April 25, 2023, raising $500 million through the sale of 50,000,000 units at $10.00 per unit. Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-half of one redeemable warrant.

Private Placement: Concurrently with the IPO, a private placement of 14,300,000 warrants was completed, generating $14.3 million in proceeds. These warrants are exercisable to purchase Class A ordinary shares at $11.50 per share.

Trust Account: A total of $505 million from the IPO proceeds, private placement, and overfunding loans was placed in a Trust Account, intended to be used for the initial business combination.

Acquisition Strategy: The company aims to identify and acquire businesses with a differentiated and sustainable business model, strong growth prospects, and the potential to benefit from being publicly traded. The strategy leverages Ares Management's platform and network.

Future Outlook: The company has until April 25, 2025, to complete its initial business combination, with the possibility of extending this period subject to shareholder approval. If unable to complete a business combination within this timeframe, the company will redeem 100% of its public shares.

Operational Flexibility: The company plans to use cash, equity, debt, or a combination thereof to effectuate its business combination, providing flexibility in structuring the transaction to meet the needs of the target business.

Emerging Growth Company Status: As an emerging growth company, Ares Acquisition Corporation II benefits from reduced reporting requirements and intends to take advantage of the extended transition period for complying with new or revised accounting standards.

Competition: The company faces competition from other blank check companies, private equity groups, and strategic acquirers, many of which have greater resources and experience in identifying and completing business combinations.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Ares Acquisition Corporation II is focused on completing an initial business combination by April 25, 2025, using proceeds from its Initial Public Offering and Private Placement Warrants. The company is actively seeking shareholder approval to extend the Combination Period to ensure sufficient time for this strategic initiative.

Capital Management: The company raised $500 million through its Initial Public Offering and an additional $14.3 million from the Private Placement of Warrants. These funds, along with overfunding loans, were placed in a Trust Account and invested in U.S. government securities. The company has not paid any dividends and does not plan to do so before completing a business combination. Additionally, the company has no long-term debt obligations or off-balance sheet arrangements.

Future Outlook: Ares Acquisition Corporation II plans to complete its initial business combination before the mandatory liquidation of the Trust Account. The company is considering the impact of inflation, interest rate fluctuations, and geopolitical events on its ability to finalize a business combination. Management is prepared to seek additional financing from the Sponsor or its affiliates if necessary to meet obligations through the completion of the business combination.

Challenges and Risks

Early Stage Company Risks: The company is in its early stages with no revenues, making it difficult to evaluate its ability to achieve business objectives. This uncertainty poses a high risk to investors.

Cybersecurity Risks: Potential security incidents or cyber-attacks could adversely affect the company's business, financial condition, and operating results.

Shareholder Voting and Redemption Risks: Shareholders may not have the opportunity to vote on the initial business combination, and the redemption rights could make the financial condition unattractive to potential business combination targets.

Going Concern Doubts: There is substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern, as highlighted by the independent registered public accounting firm's report.

Regulatory and Compliance Risks: Changes in laws or regulations, including the new 2024 SPAC Rules, may adversely affect the company's ability to negotiate and complete its initial business combination.

Market and Economic Risks: Fluctuations in inflation and interest rates, as well as geopolitical tensions, could make it more difficult for the company to consummate an initial business combination.

Investment Company Act Risks: If deemed an investment company, the company may face burdensome compliance requirements and restrictions on its activities, hindering its ability to complete a business combination.

Competition for Business Combinations: The company faces intense competition from other entities with similar business objectives, which may limit its ability to successfully negotiate a business combination.

Financial and Operational Risks Post-Combination: Post-business combination, the company may face risks related to the management of the acquired business, potential write-downs, and impairment charges.

Management's Discussion and Analysis: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the current market conditions and regulatory environment. Strategies to address these challenges include seeking shareholder approval to extend the combination period and exploring potential business combination opportunities that align with the company's strategic objectives.

Market Risk Disclosures: The company is exposed to market risks, including foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations and interest rate volatility, which may adversely affect its financial results. Management is considering various financial instruments and strategies to mitigate these risks.

SEC Filing: Ares Acquisition Corp II [ AACT ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2025

data.title: AUDIOEYE INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
AudioEye Inc., a leading digital accessibility platform, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, detailing significant financial growth, strategic initiatives, and the challenges faced in the rapidly evolving digital accessibility industry. The company, known for its patented technology that enhances website accessibility compliance, primarily generates revenue through SaaS subscriptions. The report highlights AudioEye's commitment to expanding its market presence and improving financial performance while navigating regulatory and competitive landscapes.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $35.2 million, increased by 12% over the prior year driven by growth in both Partner and Marketplace and Enterprise channels.
  • Gross Profit: $27.9 million, increased by 15% over the prior year due to increased revenue.
  • Operating Loss: $3.4 million, improved by 43% from the prior year due to increased revenue and cost efficiencies.
  • Net Loss: $4.3 million, improved by 28% from the prior year, reflecting increased revenue and cost management.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: AudioEye provides website accessibility compliance solutions to businesses of all sizes, using patented technology to enhance accessibility and user experience.
  • Revenue Segments: The company operates through two primary sales channels: the Partner and Marketplace channel, which serves small and medium-sized businesses, and the Enterprise channel, which includes larger organizations and government agencies. The Partner and Marketplace channel accounted for 58% of the Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) at the end of 2024, while the Enterprise channel represented 42%.
  • Customer Base: As of December 31, 2024, AudioEye had approximately 127,000 customers, up from 110,000 in 2023.
  • Competitive Strengths: The company's competitive strengths include its unique patented technology, a combination of automated and expert-driven services, broad price points, and a highly experienced team. AudioEye emphasizes transparency and reliability in its offerings.
  • Legal and Regulatory Framework: AudioEye's solutions help businesses comply with various accessibility laws, including the ADA and WCAG standards. The company is prepared for upcoming regulations, such as the European Accessibility Act, which will take effect in June 2025.
  • Future Outlook: AudioEye plans to continue investing in research and development to expand its intellectual property portfolio and enhance its product offerings. The company expects to see continued growth in recurring revenue and aims to improve cash flow from operating activities in 2025.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Growth: In 2024, AudioEye focused on strategic growth through the acquisition of ADA Site Compliance, which contributed to increased revenue in the Enterprise channel.
  • Capital Management: The company initiated an At The Market (ATM) offering, raising $6.6 million to support its strategic initiatives. Additionally, AudioEye engaged in several capital management activities, including a share repurchase program authorized in November 2023, which was terminated in March 2025 after repurchasing $2.02 million worth of shares. A new share repurchase program was authorized in January 2025 for up to $12.5 million through January 2027.
  • Liquidity Management: AudioEye maintained $5.7 million in cash and cash equivalents as of December 31, 2024, and a working capital of $549,000. The company also managed a $7.0 million term loan maturing in 2026.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, AudioEye plans to continue investing in product development and sales and marketing to capture market demand. The company anticipates improved cash flow from operating activities in 2025, driven by expected revenue growth. AudioEye remains open to raising additional capital if necessary to support its strategic goals and manage unforeseen events.

Challenges and Risks

  • Regulatory Compliance: AudioEye faces challenges in maintaining compliance with various U.S. and international accessibility laws, such as the ADA and the European Accessibility Act, which will take effect in June 2025. Continuous updates to products are necessary to align with these regulations.
  • Profitability Concerns: The company has a history of significant losses and may not achieve profitability, impacting its ability to continue operations.
  • Sales Channel Risks: Reliance on various sales channels may not yield expected benefits, and additional capital is required to support future development.
  • Debt Management: The $7.0 million loan due in November 2026 poses a risk if financial covenants are not met.
  • Market and Competitive Risks: Global economic conditions and geopolitical developments could adversely affect the company's industry and results. Intense competition from larger, better-financed competitors could impact AudioEye's market position.
  • Acquisition Risks: The acquisition of ADA Site Compliance presents integration risks, and the company may not realize expected benefits.
  • Intellectual Property Protection: Failure to protect intellectual property rights could negatively impact operations.
  • Operational Risks: The company is exposed to risks related to system interruptions, security breaches, and evolving government regulations.

SEC Filing: AUDIOEYE INC [ AEYE ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2025

The Fundraising will launch immediately following the release of this Announcement. Research Capital Corporation (“RCC”) is acting as sole agent and sole bookrunner (the “Agent”) in connection with the Prospectus Offering. Tennyson Securities, a trading name of Shard Capital Partners LLP (“Tennyson Securities”) and Hannam & Partners, a trading name of H&P Advisory Limited (“H&P”) are acting as joint bookrunners (the “Joint Bookrunners”) in connection with the Placing.

Overview of the Fundraising

  • Southern intends to conduct an approximate US$6.0 million equity fundraise to accelerate the completion of its three drilled and uncompleted (“DUC”) wells, drilled as part of its Q1 2023 drilling campaign on its Gwinville acreage, as well as fully funding (alongside cashflow) the drilling of two vertical Cotton Valley wells on its Mechanicsburg acreage;
  • The Company expects to benefit from strengthening U.S. natural gas prices in the near-term, currently > US$4.40/MMbtu (equivalent to C$6.40/MMbtu), further supporting the timing for completion of the DUC wells;
  • The accelerated development program in Gwinville and Mechanicsburg is anticipated to be accretive to Southern through the addition of > US$20.0 million (1) in proved developed producing (“PDP”) NPV10 value;
  • Based on type curve estimates, the Company expects the Gwinville DUCs, once completed, to have initial production (IP30) rates of approximately 5.5 MMcf/d per well, with expected ultimate recovery per well of approximately 3.5 Bcfe, while the Mechanicsburg wells are expected to have IP30 rates of approximately 4.2 MMcf/d plus 75 bbl/d of liquids per well, with ultimate recovery per well of approximately 3.7 Bcfe;
  • The Gwinville DUCs are expected to have an IRR of 86% (2) while the new Mechanicsburg wells are expected to have an IRR of 77%;
  • Completion of the Gwinville DUCs and Mechanicsburg drilling is expected to begin in Q2 2025 and will provide Southern with a significant platform for organic growth, with production expected to reach > 4,000 boepd by year-end 2025, representing approximately 100% growth;
  • The Company has identified over 100 additional horizontal drilling locations at Gwinville which it will target for development in appropriate gas price environments. Future wells are expected to achieve a ~ 30% IRR at a natural gas price of US$3.75/MMBtu;
  • The net proceeds from the Fundraising are expected to fully fund, alongside existing cash, cash flows and undrawn debt facilities, the completion of the Gwinville DUCs at a cost of approximately US$2.5 million per well and the drilling of the Mechanicsburg wells at a cost of approximately US$3.5 million;
  • The Company has executed an amendment to the Credit Facility to reduce monthly principal amortization payments to approximately 15% per annum, which is expected to free up more than $2.5 million over the remaining term of the loan to support continued organic growth;
  • The Company intends to seek the approval of the holders of its outstanding convertible unsecured subordinated debentures (the "Debentures"), by way of obtaining extraordinary resolutions of greater than 66.67% of the aggregate principal amount of the Debentures, to amend the terms of the indenture governing the Debentures such that, subject to and concurrent with the completion of the Fundraising, an amount equal to 102.5% of the principal amount outstanding under the Debentures plus all accrued and unpaid interest as of the closing date would convert into Units at the Prospectus Price (the "Debenture Amendment"). The completion of the Debenture Amendment and the issuance of the Units upon the conversion of the Debentures remain subject to acceptance of the TSXV. The Units to be issued pursuant to the Debenture Amendment will be subject to customary lock-up provisions.

Ian Atkinson, President and CEO of Southern, commented:

“I am pleased to announce the launch of our proposed fundraising today, which will accelerate Southern’s work program, starting with the completion of the three DUC wells in our Gwinville acreage in Mississippi. After pausing our previous development program at the end of Q1 2023, we are excited to resume operations at Gwinville to capitalize on the significant uptick in Henry Hub natural gas pricing and bring this high-impact production on-line. Current Henry Hub natural gas pricing is very supportive with balance of 2025 and calendar year 2026 averaging greater than US$4.80/MMBtu and US$4.40/MMBtu, respectively. In addition, Southern continues to receive strong basis premium pricing that was approximately 15% higher on average than Henry Hub in January and February 2025. We’ve made a significant capital investment in these wells, and we are now positioned to realize substantial cash flow generation as we rapidly ramp up production in a short timeframe alongside a very constructive natural gas price.

“We continue to see an increasingly positive macro environment for the U.S. natural gas market, and we believe that Southern is firmly positioned to capitalize on the opportunity presented to us through this structural imbalance. Today’s capital raise will enable us to increase our exposure to the opportunity, as we see gas prices react to increasing LNG export capacity from the U.S. Gulf Coast region, increasing natural gas fired power consumption, and seasonal demand factors. I look forward to allocating the raised capital to our portfolio of highly productive and profitable assets, and increasing shareholder value as we enter a resurgent U.S. natural gas market.”

READER ADVISORY

Currency. All currency amounts in this Announcement are in United States dollars (unless otherwise stated).

Disclosure of Oil and Gas Information

Unit Cost Calculation. Natural gas liquids volumes are recorded in barrels of oil (bbl) and are converted to a thousand cubic feet equivalent (Mcfe) using a ratio of six (6) thousand cubic feet to one (1) barrel of oil (bbl). Natural gas volumes recorded in thousand cubic feet (Mcf) are converted to barrels of oil equivalent (boe) using the ratio of six (6) thousand cubic feet to one (1) barrel of oil (bbl). Mcfe and boe may be misleading, particularly if used in isolation. A boe conversion ratio of 6 mcf:1 bbl or a Mcfe conversion ratio of 1 bbl:6 Mcf is based in an energy equivalency conversion method primarily applicable at the burner tip and does not represent a value equivalency at the wellhead. In addition, given that the value ratio based on the current price of oil as compared with natural gas is significantly different from the energy equivalent of six to one, utilizing a boe conversion ratio of 6 Mcf:1 bbl or a Mcfe conversion ratio of 1 bbl:6 Mcf may be misleading as an indication of value.

Product Types. References to “natural gas” throughout this Announcement refer to conventional natural gas as defined by National Instrument 51-101 – Standards of Disclosure for Oil and Gas Activities (“NI 51-101”). References to “liquids” throughout this Announcement comprise pentane, butane, propane, and ethane, being all natural gas liquids as defined by NI 51-101.

Reserves Disclosure. All reserves values, NPV10 and ancillary information contained in this Announcement was prepared by the Company's internal qualified reserves evaluator (“QRE”) based on 3 February 2025 strip pricing in accordance with NI 51-101 and the most recent publication of the Canadian Oil and Gas Evaluation Handbook (“COGEH”), unless otherwise noted. All reserve references in this Announcement are “company gross reserves”. Estimates of reserves for individual properties may not reflect the same level of confidence as estimates of reserves for all properties, due to the effect of aggregation.

Proved reserves are those reserves that can be estimated with a high degree of certainty to be recoverable. It is likely that the actual remaining quantities recovered will exceed the estimated proved reserves. Probable reserves are those additional reserves that are less certain to be recovered than proved reserves. It is equally likely that the actual remaining quantities recovered will be greater or less than the sum of the estimated proved plus probable reserves. Proved developed producing reserves are those reserves that are expected to be recovered from completion intervals open at the time of the estimate. These reserves may be currently producing or, if shut-in, they must have previously been on production, and the date of resumption of production must be known with reasonable certainty. Undeveloped reserves are those reserves expected to be recovered from known accumulations where a significant expenditure (e.g., when compared to the cost of drilling a well) is required to render them capable of production. They must fully meet the requirements of the reserves category (proved, probable, possible) to which they are assigned. Certain terms used in this Announcement but not defined are defined in NI 51-101, CSA Staff Notice 51-324 – Revised Glossary to NI 51-101, Revised Glossary to NI 51-101, Standards of Disclosure for Oil and Gas Activities (“CSA Staff Notice 51-324”) and/or the COGEH and, unless the context otherwise requires, shall have the same meanings herein as in NI 51-101, CSA Staff Notice 51-324 and the COGEH, as the case may be.

Type Curves. Type curve disclosure presented herein represents estimates of the production decline and ultimate volumes expected to be recovered from wells over the life of the well. The reservoir engineering and statistical analysis methods utilized are broad and can include various methods of technical decline analyses, and reservoir simulation all of which are generally prescribed and accepted by the most recent publication of the COGHE and widely accepted reservoir engineering practices. These type curves were generated by Southern's internal QRE and incorporate the most recent data from actual well results and would only be representative of the specific drilled locations. There is no guarantee that Southern will achieve the estimated or similar results derived therefrom. Individual wells may be higher or lower but over a larger number of wells, management expects the average to come out to the type curve. Over time type curves can and will change based on achieving more production history on older wells or more recent completion information on newer wells.

Drilling Locations. This Announcement discloses drilling locations in two categories: (i) booked locations; and (ii) unbooked locations. Booked locations are derived from the report prepared by Netherland, Sewell & Associates, Inc. dated February 20, 2024 and effective as of December 31, 2023 evaluating the crude oil, natural gas and natural gas liquids reserves of Southern and account for drilling locations that have associated proved and/or probable reserves, as applicable. Unbooked locations are internal estimates by the Company’s QRE based on the prospective acreage and an assumption as to the number of wells that can be drilled per section based on industry practice and internal review. Unbooked locations do not have attributed reserves or resources. There is no certainty that the Company will drill all unbooked drilling locations and if drilled there is no certainty that such locations will result in additional oil and gas reserves, resources or production. The drilling locations considered for future development will ultimately depend upon the availability of capital, regulatory approvals, seasonal restrictions, oil and natural gas prices, costs, actual drilling results, additional reservoir information that is obtained and other factors. While certain of the unbooked drilling locations have been derisked by drilling existing wells in relative close proximity to such unbooked drilling locations, other unbooked drilling locations are farther away from existing wells where management has less information about the characteristics of the reservoir and therefore there is more uncertainty whether wells will be drilled in such locations and if drilled there is more uncertainty that such wells will result in additional oil and gas reserves, resources or production.

Short Term Results. References in this press release to peak rates, initial production rates, IP30 and other short-term production rates are useful in confirming the presence of hydrocarbons, however such rates are not determinative of the rates at which such wells will commence production and decline thereafter and are not indicative of long-term performance or of ultimate recovery. While encouraging, readers are cautioned not to place reliance on such rates in calculating the aggregate production of Southern.

Glossary

“1P” proved reserves

“boepd” barrels of oil equivalent per day

“IP30” average hydrocarbon production rate for the first 30 days of a well's life

“LNG” liquidified natural gas

“Mcfe” thousand cubic feet equivalent

“MMboe” million barrels of oil equivalent

“MMBtu” million British thermal units

“NPV10” net present value at a 10% discount rate (before tax)

“NTM” next twelve months

“PDP” proved developed producing reserves

“PUD” proved, undeveloped

data.title: Australia Shares Set to Pare Recent Losses — Market Talk
data.description: Australia's S&P/ASX 200 looks set to pare its recent heavy losses when the local share market opens Thursday. ASX futures are up by about 0.2% ahead of the open, suggesting that the benchmark index will bounce following a session in which it briefly entered correction territory. The ASX 200 ended Wednesday 1.3% lower, 9.0% below the record close it set less than four weeks ago. Yancoal and Perpetual are among the stocks set to trade ex-dividend. Major U.S. indices provided a mixed lead for the region. The S&P 500 rose 0.5% and the Nasdaq Composite gained 1.2%, but the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.2% for a third straight decline. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: ACCLIVUS CELEBRATES 7 YEARS OF TRAILBLAZING CVI, HOSPITAL RESPONSE WORK
data.description:
Chicago, March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — With the recently proposed State budget and the City’s set goal of under 500 shootings this year, Acclivus Inc., a pioneering community health organization proficient in community violence intervention (CVI), marks its seventh anniversary of transformative work in hospital response, street outreach, behavioral health, technical training, workforce development, and partner initiatives. Viewing violence through a public health lens, Acclivus is the largest local, black-led CVI organization — made possible through collaborations with community residents, stakeholders and influencers — to transform high-risk neighborhoods by providing free resources to victims, survivors, and their families.

The backbone of Acclivus is its response to violence victims which is done in partnership with six local hospitals – Holy Cross, Insight, Mount Sinai, Northwestern Memorial, St. Bernard and John H. Stroger of Cook County.  During the often chaotic moments immediately following a violent situation when victims and their loved ones arrive at the hospital, Acclivus’ responders and case managers are onsite to de-escalate the situation, provide advocacy, and act as a resource to prevent further violence and reduce the impact of trauma.

“We have the life and professional experiences to earn the trust of victims, their families and the hospital staff,” said LeVon Stone, Sr., president and CEO of Acclivus. “I got my CVI start in hospital response work – serving as hospital responder, case manager, and director of all Illinois programs for CeaseFire; so, I understand how critically unique this emotional and social support is for all involved in the violence journey.”

Stone is a testament to the importance of lived experiences in successful violence prevention: he is a gunshot survivor, justice-impacted individual who earned two college degrees during challenging times. This relatability is key in positively influencing youth – particularly, black youth – from Acclivus’ nine street outreach communities: Altgeld Gardens, Douglas, Fuller Park, Grand Boulevard, Greater Grand Crossing, Harvey, Washington Heights, Washington Park and West Pullman.

The organization’s dedication to violence prevention extends beyond monitoring neighborhood hotspots and extends to vital efforts the CVI industry hasn’t tried consistently or intentionally: looking to and working with the community to lead violence prevention work with an understanding that it will take time to arrive at the right/best/most impactful answers.

Over the years, Acclivus has significantly expanded its impact and resources to include technical training, workforce development, youth mentoring, and program initiatives such as Peacekeepers with key partners; and has grown its annual budget from $1 million (seeded from the Michael Reese Health Trust Foundation) to over $25 million. But, its deep immersion in the work has prevented Acclivus from taking time to celebrate its remarkable achievements – most notably, the mediation of 1,200+ conflicts in the second half of 2024 - making a profound difference in the lives of countless individuals and families.

Additional Acclivus highlights include:

  • Recipient of three “first-time” grants – a $6.4M grant from the City of Chicago to serve as CVI co-lead in near-south and far-south neighborhoods; $500K from the Chicago Department of Public Health’s first ever hospital response/violence prevention grant; and $800K from the Cook County Department of Public Health for behavioral health services.
  • Hospital Response - Acclivus has provided immediate support to more than 12,000 victims of violence in hospital settings, helping to reduce retaliation and further incidents of violence.
  • Behavioral Health Services - More than 150 clients, program participants and staff have received mental health support – including individual and group counseling - aiding in their recovery and reducing the likelihood of future violence.
  • Violence Prevention Professional Training - In 2024, Acclivus provided twenty-two 40-hour trainings - covering street outreach, case management and victim advocacy core competencies – for more than 100 participants.

Acclivus is also committed to empowering other community organizations led by indigenous CEOs. By providing funding support and resources, Acclivus has helped 42 organizations build capacity and enhance their impact. This collaborative approach ensures that diverse voices and experiences are represented at the highest levels of leadership, fostering more inclusive and effective CVI efforts.

In addition to the grant funding from the City of Chicago and Cook County, Acclivus’ innovative approach and significant impact have garnered support from the Illinois Criminal Justice Information Authority (ICJIA), Illinois Department of Human Services, the Illinois Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity, and the University of Illinois at Chicago Social Work Policy Center.

“We are humble yet incredibly proud of the progress we’ve made and the lives we’ve enhanced over the years,” said Stone. “Our success is a testament to the power of a public health approach to violence prevention; a dedicated team; growing support from the state, county and city; and our ability to sustain severe budget cuts to attempt to keep smaller organizations afloat. We appeal to leaders to continue making investments in organizations, programs and initiatives that make a real difference in creating safer, healthier communities.”

Acclivus (ac·cli·vus) is a community health organization that looks at violence through a public health lens by offering several free services and resources designed to prevent, de-escalate and mitigate violence. Founded in 2010 and reactivated in 2018, Acclivus provides hospital response, street outreach, behavioral health, technical training, workforce development and mentoring assistance, as well as partner initiatives to build safe, healthy, economically-thriving communities. For more information, visit www.acclivusinc.org, call 312-766-7145, or follow @acclivusinc on Facebook, Instagram, X/Twitter and LinkedIn.

# # #

Katrina Waddy

Acclivus, Inc.

3125457392

katrina.waddy@acclivusinc.org

data.title: Brilliant Earth Q4 Adjusted Earnings Unchanged, Sales Decline; Shares Rise After Hours
data.description:
Brilliant Earth Group reported Q4 adjusted earnings late Wednesday of $0.04 per diluted share, unchanged from a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet, on average, expected to break even.

Net sales for the quarter ended Dec. 31 fell to $119.5 million from $124.3 million a year earlier.

Analysts expected $116.8 million.

For Q1, Brilliant Earth is projecting net sales between $93.5 million and $95.5 million. The firm expects its 2025 net sales to grow between 1% and 3% over $422.2 million in sales during 2024.

Analysts are looking for $96.7 million and $423.8 million, respectively.

Brilliant Earth shares were up over 15% during Wednesday's after-hours session.
